research.interface: NerdGraph GraphQL API over HTTPS POST (api.newrelic.com/graphql, api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql), actor.entitySearch cursor pagination - current GA surface

official_sources.title: Meet NerdGraph: our GraphQL API | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the regional GraphQL endpoints https://api.newrelic.com/graphql (US) and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql (EU); authentication via the API-Key header carrying a user API key

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tutorial: View entity data | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the actor.entitySearch(query:) { results { entities nextCursor } } shape; the 200-entity cap per response; cursor pagination by requesting nextCursor and passing its value to results(cursor:); nextCursor null when exhausted; entity fields guid, name, entityType, domain, accountId, reporting and tags {key values}

official_sources.title: NerdGraph usage limits | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the guaranteed limit of 25 concurrent requests per user, tracked per user rather than per key, with excess requests rejected as HTTP 429

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tutorial: handle concurrency limits | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: confirmation that clients must expect HTTP 429 when the 25-concurrent-request limit is exceeded and should bound their own concurrency to avoid it

research.language_rationale: The inventory tool runs inside our Node 26 platform tooling. New Relic documents NerdGraph as a plain GraphQL-over-HTTPS endpoint and publishes no official server-side JavaScript SDK for it, so idiomatic TypeScript with the built-in fetch and hand-written GraphQL documents is the credible integration surface - the same wire calls the NerdGraph API explorer issues.

verified_facts: NerdGraph is served at region-specific endpoints: https://api.newrelic.com/graphql for US accounts and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql for EU accounts; requests are HTTPS POSTs with a JSON body of {query, variables}.
Authentication is a New Relic user API key sent in the API-Key request header on every query and mutation.

actor.entitySearch caps a single response at 200 entities; larger inventories are traversed by selecting results.nextCursor and passing its value back through the cursor argument until nextCursor is null.
Entity search results expose guid, name, entityType, domain, accountId, reporting and tags (key/values pairs) on matching entities.
NerdGraph guarantees at most 25 concurrent requests per user (tracked per user, not per key) and rejects excess requests with HTTP status 429.

The default NerdGraph query timeout is 5 seconds; slow resolutions surface as GraphQL errors whose extensions carry an errorClass such as TIMEOUT, and GraphQL responses may combine partial data with a top-level errors array.
